task_yaml.task:
You are a graduate student preparing to choose a paper reproduction topic. Your topic is grounding / attribution / citation quality in RAG, and you care most about whether it is worth spending one week on reproduction. Use only the browser and webpages to complete the following task: find a paper published in 2026 or later on a paper website whose topic is clearly related to RAG grounding / attribution / citation quality; then find its official or author-provided GitHub repository page, and collect the stars, open issues count, most recent code update time, and whether the README provides training or inference instructions.

Write the final conclusion to:

    /tmp_workspace/results/rag_grounding_paper_review.md

The Markdown must include six top-level sections:
- paper title
- paper link
- publication venue/year
- repo link
- repo activity summary
- reproduction readiness judgment

The final conclusion must explicitly answer: "Is it worth spending one week trying to reproduce: yes / no / maybe", and give a reason of no more than 120 words. Do not only write a summary, and do not choose a pure generation paper unrelated to general RAG retrieval.
